query: Wikipedia opinion polling 2026 Brazilian presidential election latest polls May 2026
answer:
Excellent! I now have comprehensive data. Let me compile my research findings.

## Research Report: Opinion Polling for the 2026 Brazilian Presidential Election (May 2026)

### Key Background

The 2026 Brazilian presidential election has crystallized into a two-way race between **President Lula da Silva (PT)** and **Flávio Bolsonaro (PL)** — the son of former president Jair Bolsonaro, who is currently in prison (arrested November 22, 2025, sentenced to 27 years for leading a coup plot). **Tarcísio de Freitas** (previously the leading right-wing candidate) is **legally barred** from running because he chose to remain as Governor of São Paulo to seek reelection. Flávio Bolsonaro announced his candidacy in December 2025, stating he was endorsed by his father during a prison visit.

Other notable candidates include:
- **Ronaldo Caiado (PSD)** — selected by PSD as candidate on March 30, 2026
- **Romeu Zema (Novo)** — Governor of Minas Gerais
- **Daciolo/Santos (Missão/Mission)**
- **Augusto Cury (Avante)** — writer, candidacy announced April 5, 2026

**Major event in May 2026**: On May 13, leaked audio recordings linked Flávio Bolsonaro to Daniel Vorcaro, central figure in the Banco Master scandal (allegedly negotiating US$24 million / R$134 million for a film about Jair Bolsonaro's 2018 campaign). This appears to have hurt Flávio's numbers in subsequent polls.

---

### Latest First-Round Polls (May 2026)

| Pollster | Period | Lula (PT) | F. Bolsonaro (PL) | Caiado (PSD) | Zema (Novo) | Santos (Mission) | Cury (Avante) | Others | Blank/Null/Undec. | MoE | Sample |
|----------|--------|-----------|-------------------|--------------|-------------|------------------|---------------|--------|-------------------|-----|--------|
| **Datafolha** | 20–22 May | **40%** | 31% | 4% | 3% | 3% | 2% | 6% | 12% | ±2pp | 2,004 |
| **Futura** | 15–20 May | **42.7%** | 35.6% | 3.3% | 3.9% | 1.7% | 0.9% | 1.4% | 10.6% | ±2.2pp | 2,000 |
| **AtlasIntel** | 13–18 May | **47%** | 34.3% | 2.7% | 5.2% | 6.9% | 0.4% | 0.2% | 3.3% | ±1pp | 5,032 |
| **Datafolha** | 12–13 May | **38%** | 35% | 3% | 3% | 2% | 2% | 4% | 12% | ±2pp | 2,004 |
| **Quaest** | 8–11 May | **39%** | 33% | 4% | 4% | 2% | 1% | 2% | 15% | ±2pp | 2,004 |
| **Futura** | 4–8 May | 38.1% | 37.4% | 5.7% | — | 2.3% | 1.5% | 2.1% | 12.9% | ±2.2pp | 2,000 |
| **Ideia** | 1–5 May | **40%** | 36% | 5.6% | 3% | 1.4% | 1.5% | 3.4% | 9.1% | ±2.5pp | 1,500 |
| **Real Time Big Data** | 2–4 May | **40%** | 34% | 5% | 4% | 3% | 1% | 2% | 11% | ±2pp | 2,000 |

---

### Latest Second-Round (Runoff) Polls (May 2026)

| Pollster | Period | Lula vs. F. Bolsonaro | Lula vs. Caiado | Lula vs. Zema | Lead |
|----------|--------|----------------------|-----------------|---------------|------|
| **Datafolha** (20–22 May) | Lula 47% vs Bolsonaro 43% | Lula 48% vs Caiado 39% | Lula 48% vs Zema 39% | 4–9 pts |
| **Futura** (15–20 May) | Lula 47.7% vs Bolsonaro 42.2% | Lula 47.6% vs Caiado 36.5% | Lula 48.3% vs Zema 35.9% | 5.5–12.4 pts |
| **AtlasIntel** (13–18 May) | Lula 48.9% vs Bolsonaro 41.8% | Lula 47.5% vs Caiado 38.5% | Lula 47.8% vs Zema 37.6% | 7.1–10.2 pts |
| **Datafolha** (12–13 May) | **Lula 45% vs Bolsonaro 45% (TIE)** | Lula 46% vs Caiado 39% | Lula 46% vs Zema 40% | 0–7 pts |
| **Quaest** (8–11 May) | Lula 42% vs Bolsonaro 41% | Lula 44% vs Caiado 35% | Lula 44% vs Zema 37% | 1–9 pts |
| **Futura** (4–8 May) | **Bolsonaro 46.9% vs Lula 44.4%** | Lula 45.1% vs Caiado 36.9% | Lula 46% vs Zema 37.8% | -2.5 to 8.2 pts |
| **Ideia** (1–5 May) | **Bolsonaro 45.3% vs Lula 44.7%** | Lula 44.7% vs Caiado 40% | Lula 44% vs Zema 39% | -0.6 to 5 pts |
| **Real Time Big Data** (2–4 May) | **Bolsonaro 44% vs Lula 43%** | Lula 43% vs Caiado 42% | — | -1 to 1 pts |

---

### Key Trends Observed

1. **Banco Master scandal impact**: The May 13 leaked audio scandal clearly damaged Flávio Bolsonaro. Before the leak (early May), polls showed tight races or even Bolsonaro leads in runoff scenarios. After the leak, Lula opened up consistent leads of 4–7+ points in second-round matchups.

2. **First-round dominance**: Lula consistently leads in first-round polling with 38–47%, while Flávio Bolsonaro ranges from 31–37.4%. The gap appears to have widened after the scandal.

3. **Third candidates far behind**: Caiado (3–6%), Zema (3–5%), and others are polling in single digits, making the race essentially bipolar.

4. **Runoff competitiveness**: Before the scandal, several polls showed statistical ties or even slight Bolsonaro leads in head-to-head matchups. Post-scandal, Lula has moved ahead consistently.

5. **AtlasIntel vs other pollsters**: AtlasIntel tends to show larger leads for Lula (likely due to larger sample sizes of ~5,000 and different methodology).

---
